title: 10 best herbal capsules for alzheimer’s disease
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for Alzheimer's disease are natural supplements made from a blend of herbs, botanicals, and plant extracts specifically designed to support brain health and potentially alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease. These capsules help by improving memory, cognitive function, and overall brain function, reducing the risk of degeneration and inflammation associated with the condition. Examples include ginkgo biloba, turmeric, and green tea extract, which have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function in individuals with mild cognitive impairment or early-stage Alzheimer's disease. By using these herbal capsules, individuals can potentially slow down disease progression, enhance quality of life, and maintain independence for a longer period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. The capsules' active ingredients, such as californianum extract, have been found to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are key contributing factors to the progression of Alzheimer's. Additionally, maidenhair tree extracts have been demonstrated to increase blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal activity and potentially slowing down cognitive decline associated with the disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba extracts have potent antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the brain, which is thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Vasodilatory effects: Maidenhair Tree capsules can improve blood flow to the brain by dilating blood vessels, which may help restore cognitive function and memory loss associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective compounds: Ginkgo biloba extracts contain a range of neuroprotective compounds, including flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, that have been shown to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as saponins and alkaloids, which have been shown to have neuroprotective effects against Alzheimer's disease.

Barks: The barks contain a unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been demonstrated to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves and clean them thoroughly ensuring absence of contamination.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with excipients such as gelatin and water in a 1:2 ratio to form a uniform blend.
Fill the blend mixture into vegetarian capsules and seal them tightly ensuring proper encapsulation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a natural extract of Bacopa monnieri, a plant that has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to enhance cognitive function and memory. The capsules are believed to promote the growth of new neurons and improve communication between brain cells, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as memory loss, confusion, and difficulty with problem-solving and language skills.
remedy_properties: Nootropic activity: Brahmi capsules have been shown to possess nootropic properties, which improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing neurotransmitter production and transmission, thereby helping to slow down cognitive decline in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Brahmi capsules help protect the brain from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease and related cognitive decline.

Neuroprotective activity: Brahmi capsules have been found to exhibit neuroprotective effects, which help safeguard against neuronal damage and death, potentially slowing down or halting the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function in patients with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a rich source of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems in the early morning for optimal potency.
Wash the plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried bacopa monnieri into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of powdered bacopa monnieri and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain curcumin, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain. These anti-inflammatory properties may help slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's by preventing the accumulation of amyloid plaques, which are characteristic of the disease. Additionally, curcumin may also improve cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and reducing symptoms such as memory loss and confusion.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Bioavailability-enhancing compounds: Turmeric capsules often contain bioavailability-enhancing compounds like piperine (black pepper extract) that increase the absorption of curcuminoids into the bloodstream, allowing them to reach the brain more effectively and have a greater therapeutic impact on cognitive function.

Neuroprotective flavonoids: Turmeric contains a range of neuroprotective flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which can help protect against beta-amyloid plaque formation and tau protein aggregation in the brain, both hallmark features of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric are used to make medicinal capsules as they contain a high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various cognitive disorders, including Alzheimer's, due to their ability to improve memory and cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Measure 100 grams of dried turmeric root (curcuma longa) powder.
Combine the turmeric powder with 200ml of distilled water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture for 20 minutes then let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth to remove solids then dry.
Fill 500mg capsules with the resulting extract using a capsule machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. These compounds, including ginsenosides and polysaccharides, may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, ginseng has been found to enhance blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal activity and potentially slowing down the degeneration of brain cells associated with this condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to Alzheimer's disease progression.

Neuroprotective property: Ginseng has been shown to exhibit neuroprotective effects by reducing beta-amyloid plaque formation, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease, and protecting against neuronal damage.

Cognitive-enhancing property: Ginseng has been found to improve cognitive function and memory in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, possibly due to its ability to increase blood flow to the brain and enhance neurotransmitter activity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginseng roots are rich in bioactive compounds like ginsenosides, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory in Alzheimer's patients.

Rhyzomes: Ginseng rhyzomes contain a higher concentration of saponins than the roots, making them useful for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help combat oxidative stress in the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh panax ginseng roots and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the cleaned panax ginseng roots in a warm air circulation oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried panax ginseng roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground panax ginseng with 100mg of silica filler and 20mg of magnesium stearate in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful antioxidant called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function. This natural compound has also been found to reduce inflammation in the brain, a common characteristic of the disease. Additionally, sage capsules have been demonstrated to increase acetylcholine levels, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in memory formation and retrieval, thereby helping to slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Rosmarinic acid: This compound has been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to neurodegeneration associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Terpenes: Terpenes have been found to improve memory and cognitive function by increasing blood flow to the brain and protecting against neuronal damage.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, may help prevent or slow the progression of Alzheimer's disease by reducing amyloid plaque formation and improving insulin signaling in the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds that have been shown to exhibit neuroprotective properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sage leaves from your garden or store-dried sage leaves for at least 30 days before use.
Dry the sage leaves by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels and letting them air dry for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried sage leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to ensure uniform size.
Mix 500mg of powdered sage with 250mg of vitamin e oil and fill gelatin capsules according to your desired dosage.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or consume within 2 weeks for optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain the essential oil of the herb, which has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function. The active compounds in rosemary, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, have been found to increase blood flow to the brain, boost neurotransmitter production, and even prevent the formation of beta-amyloid plaques - a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. This natural approach can provide an effective way to support brain health and potentially slow down or even halt the progression of this devastating condition.
remedy_properties: Carnosic acid: Carnosic acid, a polyphenolic compound found in rosemary, has been shown to prevent beta-amyloid plaques from forming and reducing existing ones, which are characteristic of Alzheimer's disease.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, another major component of rosemary, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain that can contribute to Alzheimer's development.

Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid, a triterpene compound found in rosemary, has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by reducing apoptosis (cell death) and inhibiting inflammatory pathways in the brain, which may help prevent or slow down neuronal damage associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in fatty acids, particularly oleic acid, which has been shown to improve cognitive function and memory by reducing inflammation and promoting blood flow to the brain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in amounts of one part by weight for every 4 parts of vegetable glycerin and 20 parts of distilled water.
Mix the rosmarinus officinalis leaves with vegetable glycerin and distilled water in a ratio of 1:4:20 to create an herbal extract.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 6-8 hours or overnight to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the herbal extract.
Fill gelatin capsules with 300mg of the herbal extract and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goat's rue capsules helps with Alzheimer’s disease because it has been traditionally used to improve cognitive function, memory, and mood. The herb contains a unique compound called saponin, which has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, helping to prevent the degeneration of brain cells and reduce inflammation. Additionally, goat's rue is believed to increase blood flow to the brain, allowing for more oxygen and nutrients to reach damaged areas, potentially slowing down the progression of Alzheimer’s disease.
remedy_properties: Choline: Goat's Rue capsules contain Choline, which is converted into Acetylcholine in the brain, a neurotransmitter that plays a crucial role in memory formation and can help alleviate symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Flavonoids and Phenolic Compounds: These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Triterpenoid Saponins: Goat's Rue capsules contain Triterpenoid Saponins, which may help reduce beta-amyloid plaques, a hallmark feature of Alzheimer's disease, by inhibiting the production of beta-amyloid peptides and reducing inflammation in the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds like saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, making them suitable for treating Alzheimer's disease.

Seeds: Goat's rue seeds contain a significant amount of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess cognitive-enhancing properties, improving memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh galega officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator set to 35°c for 24 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Grind 10 grams of dried galega officinalis into a fine powder using a spice grinder and store it in an airtight container.
Mix 1 gram of powdered galega officinalis with 99 grams of purified water to create a liquid extract, steeping for 30 minutes and straining through cheesecloth.
Fill #0 gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the prepared galega officinalis liquid extract and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Asiatic pennywort capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory. The herb's potent antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are common contributors to neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's. Additionally, Asiatic pennywort has been found to enhance blood flow to the brain, promoting healthy neuronal communication and potentially reversing damage caused by the disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Centella asiatica's antioxidant properties help to protect the brain from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are thought to contribute to the development and progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Neuroprotective compounds: The herb contains a range of neuroprotective compounds, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, which may help to prevent neuronal damage and death caused by Alzheimer's-related factors such as beta-amyloid plaques and tau protein aggregation.

Immunomodulatory effects: Centella asiatica has been shown to modulate the immune response in a way that may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which could slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like saponins and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in combating oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.

Rhizomes: Contain a unique combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that have been found to exhibit neuroprotective effects, potentially reducing the risk of neuronal damage and cognitive decline.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centella asiatica leaves from a clean and pesticide-free environment weighing approximately 500 grams.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and gently pat dry with a paper towel.
Air-dry the washed leaves in a shaded area for 48 hours or until they become brittle and crumble easily.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 minutes to extract maximum bioactive compounds.
Mix one part of the powdered centella asiatica with three parts of gelatin and fill empty capsules to yield 500mg capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Club moss capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain Huperzine A, a natural compound that has been shown to inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory and cognitive function. This helps to improve cognitive impairment and slow down the progression of the disease. Additionally, club moss capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, further contributing to its neuroprotective effects against Alzheimer's.
remedy_properties: Huperzine A: Inhibits acetylcholinesterase, an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter essential for memory and cognitive function, thereby increasing its levels in the brain.
Flavonoids: Exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, common underlying factors of Alzheimer's disease.
Lignans: Have been shown to possess cholinergic activity, which can help to improve memory and cognitive function by modulating the neurotransmitter acetylcholine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots of club moss are valued for their ability to combat oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Containing a unique combination of alkaloids and saponins, rhyzomes of club moss have been traditionally used to improve cognitive function and memory retention.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried huperzia serrata leaves and stems from a trusted supplier or cultivate your own organic plant.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes to ensure uniform texture.
Mix the ground herb with 10g of glycerin and 50ml of distilled water in a glass bowl, stirring continuously for 10 minutes to create an herbal extract.
Transfer the herbal extract into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or spoon, filling each capsule with approximately 250mg of the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 1 year, taking 2-3 capsules daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a potent combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have been shown to have neuroprotective properties. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of the disease. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender may also help to reduce anxiety and agitation, common symptoms associated with Alzheimer's. By addressing these underlying issues, English lavender capsules may help to slow down cognitive decline and improve overall quality of life for individuals with Alzheimer's.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lavender's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which is a key contributor to Alzheimer's disease progression.

Cholinergic activity: Lavender's cholinergic effects may help increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, which is an essential neurotransmitter that plays a role in memory formation and recall, potentially improving cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lavender's calming properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, both of which are common symptoms of Alzheimer's disease, and may also contribute to improved sleep quality, which is often disrupted in individuals with the condition.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenes, which have been shown to improve cognitive function and memory retention.
Seeds: Containing a high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that has been found to have neuroprotective effects and help slow down the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in quantities sufficient for the desired capsule quantity.
Dry the harvested flowers using shade drying or air circulation at room temperature for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder and sift through a 40-mesh sieve to remove any large particles.
Mix 200-250 mg of the powdered lavandula angustifolia with 100-150 mg of excipients such as silicon dioxide and magnesium stearate in a small bowl for uniform blending.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture using a capsule filler and seal tightly to ensure optimal storage conditions for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a herb that has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. The capsules may also help to calm inflammation in the brain, which is often associated with the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, Maypop has been shown to improve sleep quality, which is essential for memory consolidation and learning, making it a potential natural supplement to support cognitive function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop capsules have antioxidant effects that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to Alzheimer's disease progression.

GABA-enhancing properties: Maypop contains compounds that enhance GABA (Gamma-Aminobutyric Acid) activity, a neurotransmitter involved in regulating sleep, anxiety, and relaxation; this may help improve cognitive function and reduce symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Acetylcholine-boosting properties: Maypop has been shown to increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, a neurotransmitter involved in memory formation and attention; boosting acetylcholine levels can potentially slow down cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhyzomes: Rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess neuroprotective effects and improve cognitive function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried passiflora incarnata flowers from a reputable supplier or grow your own plants in a sunny spot for 6 hours daily.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using an electric grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours of harvesting to preserve potency.
Mix 250 milliliters of vodka with 1 gram of the ground passiflora incarnata powder in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly for 2 weeks to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid extract through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and setting aside for capsule filling within 30 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 200 milligrams of the extracted liquid per serving, storing them in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules help with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a natural compound called valerenic acid, which has been shown to possess neuroprotective properties. This property may help protect brain cells from damage caused by the disease, slowing down its progression and improving cognitive function. Additionally, valerian capsules have been found to increase blood flow to the brain, allowing for better oxygenation and nutrient delivery, which can help improve memory and cognitive decline associated with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help protect the brain from oxidative stress, which can contribute to Alzheimer's disease by reducing the formation of beta-amyloid plaques.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Valerian's ability to inhibit acetylcholinesterase (an enzyme that breaks down acetylcholine) may help increase acetylcholine levels in the brain, which can improve memory and cognitive function impaired by Alzheimer's disease.

GABA receptor activation: Valerian's activiation of GABA receptors can promote a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce anxiety and agitation symptoms common in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in valepotriates, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective against cognitive decline.
Rhyzomes: Containing isovaleric acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to improve memory and cognitive function in individuals with Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on low setting for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 500mg of ground valeriana officinalis with 100mg of silica gel in a small bowl for 2 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because of its potential to enhance cognitive function and memory. The herb contains compounds that have been shown to improve blood flow to the brain, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with the disease. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects may help alleviate anxiety and agitation, common symptoms of Alzheimer's. As a result, studies suggest that supplementing with herbal lemon balm capsules may slow cognitive decline and improve overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant compounds: Lemon balm capsules contain antioxidant compounds that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage brain cells and contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

GABA-like effects: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, a compound that has been shown to increase GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid) levels in the brain, which can help calm anxiety, improve sleep quality, and reduce symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.

Acetylcholinesterase inhibition: Lemon balm capsules may help increase the production of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that is often depleted in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, by inhibiting the enzyme acetylcholinesterase, which breaks down acetylcholine. This can improve cognitive function and memory.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have been traditionally used to treat cognitive impairment and memory loss due to their high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in essential oils and flavonoids that have neuroprotective effects, making them an effective ingredient for alleviating symptoms of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and dry them using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 10 grams of pure bee pollen in a bowl until well combined.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. Research suggests that hyperforin may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain, which are thought to contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease. Additionally, hyperforin may also increase levels of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, which can help regulate mood and cognitive function. This natural extract may provide a safe and effective adjunct therapy for managing mild to moderate Alzheimer's symptoms.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have neuroprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain, which can contribute to the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Hypaconitine: This alkaloid has been found to inhibit the breakdown of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that is often impaired in individuals with Alzheimer's disease, helping to improve cognitive function and memory.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort capsules have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against damage caused by free radicals in the brain and may slow down the progression of neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer's.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties, which can help mitigate the progression of Alzheimer's disease.

Buds: The buds contain high levels of hypericins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful in combating oxidative stress and inflammation associated with Alzheimer's.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves at dawn or before noon on a sunny day.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper bags or trays for 7-10 days.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered hypericum perforatum with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create a tincture solution that will take 2 weeks to steep at room temperature.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the tincture solution after 14 days and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap capsules helps with Alzheimer's disease because they contain a powerful herb that has been shown to have neuroprotective properties. The herb, Scutellaria lateriflora, has been found to increase the production of a protein called BDNF, which is essential for the growth and survival of brain cells. By promoting the health and function of these cells, skullcap capsules may help to slow down or even halt the progression of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_properties: Baicalin: Baicalin, a flavonoid compound found in skullcap, has been shown to inhibit amyloid-β protein aggregation, which is a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease. This may help prevent the formation of toxic protein clumps that can damage brain cells.

Scutellarin: Scutellarin, another flavonoid in skullcap, has been found to have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the brain associated with Alzheimer's disease.

Borneol: Borneol, a terpene compound in skullcap, has been shown to exhibit neuroprotective effects by blocking the production of inflammatory cytokines and inhibiting the activation of pro-apoptotic pathways, which can help prevent neuronal damage and death.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in combating oxidative stress associated with Alzheimer's disease.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes are rich in iridoid glycosides, particularly baicalin, which has been found to inhibit the production of beta-amyloid peptides, a hallmark of Alzheimer's disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality scutellaria baicalensis roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a hot air circulation oven at 60°c for 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 500 grams of dried scutellaria baicalensis root into a fine powder using a mill or food processor.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered root with 125 grams of potato starch and 25 grams of magnesium stearate, then compress into capsules.
Package 500 capsules in amber glass bottles, store them in a cool dark place, and use within 2 years from date of manufacture.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat Alzheimer's disease with herbal capsules is to combine standardized extracts of Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Phosphatidylserine. These herbs have been shown to improve memory, cognitive function, and brain blood flow. Take 2-3 capsules, twice daily, with food. Additionally, consider combining these herbs with omega-3 fatty acids, vitamin D, and B-complex vitamins for enhanced benefits. Consult a healthcare professional before starting any supplement regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to Alzheimer's disease that are treated with herbal capsules are Vascular Dementia, Lewy Body Disease, Frontotemporal Dementia, and Mixed Dementia. Herbal supplements such as Bacopa monnieri, Ginkgo biloba, and Phosphatidylserine have been traditionally used to improve memory, cognitive function, and neuroprotection in these conditions. These herbs may help reduce symptoms of dementia, including forgetfulness, confusion, and difficulty with communication and daily activities.
